content: The Bhagavad Gita emphasizes the importance of doing one's duty (dharma) and performing actions (karma yoga) without attachment to their outcomes [1]. It states that action is higher than inactivity and that one must always do their duty, as inaction will not even allow one to sustain their body. The Gita also advises individuals to act dispassionately and offer their actions as a sacrifice, which will help them attain freedom from the cycle of rebirth. Additionally, it highlights the interdependence of individuals and the divine, suggesting that by performing their duties and offering their actions as a sacrifice, they will receive the support and nourishment they need [1].
